# pg_procrustes

> A fast, flexible PostgreSQL SQL formatter written in Go.
> Driven by the native PostgreSQL parser (libpg_query), it understands SQL exactly like the database itself.

pg_procrustes reformats PostgreSQL SQL files according to a declarative YAML configuration.
It is idempotent: running it twice produces the same result. It handles plain SQL, DML,
DDL, CTEs, and PL/pgSQL function bodies (dollar-quoted blocks).

## Key facts

- Language: Go (CGO, uses libpg_query via pg_query_go)
- License: MIT
- Config file: `.pg_procrustes.yaml` (auto-detected from current dir upward)
- Platforms: macOS (arm64, amd64), Linux (amd64, arm64), Windows (amd64, arm64)
- Install: `brew install heptau/tap/pg-procrustes`

## What it formats

- Reserved keywords, SQL keywords, operators — uppercase / lowercase / preserve
- Data types — long form (`character varying`), short form (`varchar`), normalized
- Column, table, schema, function, alias names — casing
- Conditional functions: COALESCE, NULLIF, GREATEST, LEAST
- System functions: NOW(), CURRENT_DATE, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, etc.
- PL/pgSQL variables: NEW, OLD, TG_OP, TG_TABLE_NAME, FOUND, SQLSTATE, etc.
- PL/pgSQL keywords: RAISE, PERFORM, ELSIF, FOREACH, EXIT, LOOP, ASSERT, etc.
- Casing exceptions: specific identifiers can be pinned to exact casing
- Trailing whitespace, semicolons, blank lines
- Inequality operators: `<>` (ANSI) vs `!=` (C style)
- NOT IN vs `<> ALL` equivalence
- ASC keyword in ORDER BY: add explicit / remove redundant
- CAST(x AS t) vs `x::t` (operator style)
- JOIN qualifiers: INNER/OUTER (short vs long form)
- Schema qualification: remove `public.` prefix
- Quoted identifiers: remove safe double-quotes
- Operator spacing, comma spacing, paren spacing
- Clause breaking: each SQL clause on its own line (auto / always / never / preserve)
- Content breaking: comma-lists and AND/OR conditions (auto / always / first_inline / never)
- CASE expressions: expand or collapse WHEN/THEN/ELSE
- INSERT column list formatting
- WITH (CTE) formatting
- UNION / INTERSECT / EXCEPT blank lines
- Paren-indented blocks: close-paren position
- Dollar-quoted bodies: PL/pgSQL block structure (DECLARE/BEGIN/EXCEPTION/END)
- PL/pgSQL IF / ELSIF / ELSE blocks: indentation, blank lines
- PL/pgSQL LOOP blocks (FOR, WHILE, plain LOOP): indentation, blank lines
- PL/pgSQL CASE statements (simple and searched): indent, then-break, body-break

## CLI usage

```
pg_procrustes [flags] [file ...]
```

Flags:
- `-w`               write result back to source files
- `--check`          exit 1 if any file would change (CI / pre-commit)
- `--diff`           print unified diff without writing
- `--backup[=.ext]`  save original before overwriting (requires -w)
- `--out-dir <dir>`  write formatted files to a different directory
- `-c <path>`        explicit config file path

## Config structure (YAML)

```yaml
reserved_keywords: { case: upper }
keywords:          { case: upper }
data_types:        { case: lower, form: long }
literals:          { case: upper }
operators:         { case: upper }
schemas:           { case: lower }
tables:            { case: lower }
columns:           { case: lower }
functions:         { case: lower }
conditional_functions: { case: upper }
system_functions:      { case: upper }
aliases:           { case: lower, as: add }
plpgsql_variables: { case: upper }
plpgsql_keywords:  { case: upper }

trailing_whitespace: strip
semicolons:          preserve
inequality_op:       c          # preserve | ansi | c
join_form:           preserve   # preserve | short | long
operator_spacing:    normalize  # preserve | normalize | compact
comma_spacing:       normalize  # preserve | normalize | compact
blank_lines:         preserve   # preserve | max_3 | max_2 | max_1
paren_spacing:       remove     # preserve | add | remove
quoted_identifiers:  remove_safe
schema_qualification: preserve  # preserve | remove_public
cast_style:          preserve   # preserve | operator
order_asc:           preserve   # preserve | add | remove
not_in:              preserve   # preserve | not_in | not_equals_all

layout:
  line_length: 128
  indent:
    size: 3
    type: spaces
  clauses:
    break: auto
    align: same
  content:
    break: auto
    align: indent
    first_item: same_line
  case:
    break: preserve
    indent: indent
  union:
    blank_line: preserve   # preserve | none | before | after | both
  dollar_quote:
    newline_after_open:   preserve
    newline_before_close: preserve
    sql:
      blank_line_before: preserve
      blank_line_after:  preserve
    plpgsql:
      keyword_indent:     preserve
      declare_when_empty: preserve
      end_semicolon:      preserve
```
